name: skill-development
description: This skill should be used when the user wants to "create a skill", "add a skill to plugin", "write a new skill", "improve skill description", "organize skill content", or needs guidance on skill structure, progressive disclosure, or skill development best practices for Claude Code plugins.
capabilities: Guide users through a six-step skill creation process
Explain skill anatomy: SKILL.md, scripts, references, and assets
Teach progressive disclosure for efficient context management
Provide writing style requirements: imperative form, third-person descriptions
Show validation checklist for quality assurance
Demonstrate skill structure with real examples from plugin-dev
author: anthropics
securitySummary: Official Anthropic skill containing only markdown documentation and guidance for skill creation. No executable code, scripts, network operations, or file system access beyond reading the skill content itself. Pure documentation with zero attack surface.
valueStatement: Creating skills from scratch can lead to inconsistent structure and missed best practices. This skill provides a systematic six-step process for building effective skills with progressive disclosure, strong trigger descriptions, and proper resource organization.
userTitle: Create skills for Claude Code plugins
limitations: This skill focuses on plugin skills, not standalone skill packages
Does not include skill packaging or distribution scripts
Does not provide auto-generation of skill files from templates

useCases.title: Build new plugin skills
useCases.description: Create skills for your plugin following best practices for structure, triggers, and organization.

useCases.title: Improve existing skills
useCases.description: Refactor bloated SKILL.md files using progressive disclosure and move details to references.

useCases.title: Design skill taxonomies
useCases.description: Plan skill contents by analyzing use cases and identifying reusable scripts, references, and assets.

promptTemplates.title: Create new skill
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create a skill for [TASK]. Walk me through the six-step skill creation process.

promptTemplates.title: Refactor skill
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my existing skill at [PATH] and suggest improvements for trigger phrases, writing style, and progressive disclosure.

promptTemplates.title: Validate skill
promptTemplates.prompt: Validate my skill using the checklist. Check the description quality, body writing style, and resource organization.

promptTemplates.title: Structure guidance
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain what goes in SKILL.md versus references/ versus scripts/ for my [SKILL TYPE] skill.

bestPractices: Use third-person descriptions with specific trigger phrases: This skill should be used when the user asks to create X or configure Y.
Keep SKILL.md lean at 1,500-2,000 words and move detailed content to references/ files.
Write in imperative form: Start by reading the file. Validate input before processing.
antiPatterns: Using vague trigger phrases like provides guidance for X instead of specific user queries.
Putting all content in SKILL.md without using references/ for detailed documentation.
Writing in second person: You should do X instead of imperative: Do X.

faq.question: What is the difference between scripts, references, and assets?
faq.answer: Scripts contain executable code. References are documentation loaded as needed. Assets are files used in output like templates or images.

faq.question: What is the ideal length for SKILL.md?
faq.answer: Keep SKILL.md between 1,500-2,000 words. Move detailed content to references/ for progressive disclosure.

faq.question: How do skills work with Claude Code plugins?
faq.answer: Plugin skills live in the skills/ directory. Claude Code auto-discovers skills with SKILL.md files.

faq.question: Is user data stored when using this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance only. No data storage or transmission occurs.

faq.question: How is this skill different from the original skill-creator?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on plugin-specific skills with simpler manual structure instead of init_skill.py scripts.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for standalone skills?
faq.answer: No. This skill is designed for plugin skills distributed with Claude Code plugins, not standalone packages.
